name: Gmail Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Gmail Workflow. This workflow integrates 12 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, wait, code, scheduleTrigger. It contains 34 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: TaddyTopDaily

parameters.name: query
parameters.value: =query { getTopChartsByGenres( limitPerPage:10, filterByCountry:UNITED_STATES_OF_AMERICA, taddyType:PODCASTEPISODE, genres:PODCASTSERIES_{{ $json.genre }}){ topChartsId podcastEpisodes{ uuid name audioUrl podcastSeries{ uuid name } } } }

{{ ['Podcast', 'Episode', 'Summary'].map(propname=>'').join('') }} {{ $json.fields.map(ep=>{ return ``} ) }}

nodes.notes: This html node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Daily Podcast Summary
### This workflow will summarize the content in the day's top podcasts for a certain genre, then send you the podcasts with summaries by email

## Setup:
 1. Create a free API key on Taddy here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
 2. Input your user number and API key into the `TaddyTopDaily` node in the header parameters X-USER-ID and X-API-KEY respectively.
 3. Create access credentials for your Gmail as described here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} Use the credentials from your *client_secret.json* in the `Gmail` node.
 4. In the `Genre` node, set the genre of podcasts you want a summary for. Valid values are: TECHNOLOGY, NEWS, ARTS, COMEDY, SPORTS, FICTION, etc. Look at api.taddy.org for the full list (they will be displayed in the help docs as PODCASTSERIES_TECHNOLOGY, PODCASTSERIES_NEWS, etc.)
 5. Enter your email address in the `Gmail` node.
 6. Change the schedule time for sending email from `Schedule` to whichever time you want to receive the email.
 

## Test:
- Link a `Test Workflow` node in place of the `Schedule` node.
- Hit Test Workflow.
- Check your email for the results.

parameters.content:
## Daily Podcast Summary
### This workflow will summarize the content in the day's top podcasts for a certain genre, then send you the podcasts with summaries by email

## Setup:
 1. Create a free API key on Taddy here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
 2. Input your user number and API key into the `TaddyTopDaily` node in the header parameters X-USER-ID and X-API-KEY respectively.
 3. Create access credentials for your Gmail as described here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} Use the credentials from your *client_secret.json* in the `Gmail` node.
 4. In the `Genre` node, set the genre of podcasts you want a summary for. Valid values are: TECHNOLOGY, NEWS, ARTS, COMEDY, SPORTS, FICTION, etc. Look at api.taddy.org for the full list (they will be displayed in the help docs as PODCASTSERIES_TECHNOLOGY, PODCASTSERIES_NEWS, etc.)
 5. Enter your email address in the `Gmail` node.
 6. Change the schedule time for sending email from `Schedule` to whichever time you want to receive the email.
 

## Test:
- Link a `Test Workflow` node in place of the `Schedule` node.
- Hit Test Workflow.
- Check your email for the results.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Schedule".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Gmail" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "TaddyTopDaily" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Whisper Transcribe Audio" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HTML" node (n8n-nodes-base.html) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the AI node "Summarize Podcast".
Adjust the Model settings (e.g. gpt-4o, gpt-3.5-turbo) and write or refine the System Prompt parameters.
Open the "Request Audio Crop" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Download Link" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Download Cut MP3" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Download Podcast" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If Downloads Ready" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Genre".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Final Data".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
